steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined sentence reads: "They omitted parts, such as the aria ‘I Got Plenty of Nuthin’,’ and sometimes made only brief gestures toward Gershwin’s melodies." The sentence immediately before it states that Davis and Evans "felt no desire to please listeners expecting an exact duplication of the opera." The question asks what role the underlined sentence plays in the passage.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It presents examples to support a claim made earlier in the text.': Omitting the aria and making brief gestures toward melodies are specific examples of departing from the original — exactly supporting the claim that Davis and Evans had no desire to duplicate the opera. ✓
  • Choice B — 'It shows how two artists benefited from ignoring certain conventions.': The underlined sentence describes what they did, not any benefit they gained. The benefit (an enduring album) appears in the next sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It proposes a reason why one work of art is widely thought to be more successful than another.': The sentence does not make a comparison between the 1958 adaptation and the 1935 original, nor does it argue one is more successful. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It undermines an assertion made later in the text.': The sentence comes before, not after, the closing assertion about the album’s endurance; it does not undermine that assertion. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
